entries.definition:
strive to have them destroyed/deserted?
مُنِعَ muniʿa [v. pass.] to be denied (to) (12:63) يَاأَبَانَا مُنِعَ مِنَّا الْكَيْلُ
father, we have been denied [any more] buying [of corn].

مَانِع māniʿ [act. part.] one which causes to be impregnable,
protector (59:2) وَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُمْ مَانِعَتُهُمْ حُصُونُهُمْ and they thought their
fortifications would protect them.

مَنُوع manūʿ [intens. act. part.] one given to denying assistance,
niggardly person (70:21) وَإِذَا مَسَّهُ الْخَيْرُ مَنُوعًا but tight-fisted when
good fortune comes his way.

مَنَّاع mannāʿ [intens. act. part.] one who often stands in the way
of doing good, or denies assistance (50:25) مَنَّاعٍ لِلْخَيْرِ hinderer of
the good.

مَمْنُوعَة mamnūʿatun [pass. part.] forbidden, denied (56:33) لَا
مَقْطُوعَةٍ وَلَا مَمْنُوعَةٍ [abundant fruits] neither cut off, nor forbidden.

entries.definition:
m–n–n to cut; a weak rope, a tattered garment, fatigue; vigour,
strength of heart; death, eventualities, passage of time; to grant a
favour, to remind someone of favours you have done for them;
honey-like substance; a certain weight. مَنّ mann, honey-like
substance, is classified under this root, although it appears to be a
borrowing from either Syriac or Hebrew. Of this root, four forms
occur 27 times in the Qur’an: مَنَّ manna 16 times; مَنّ mann six
times; مَمْنُون mamnūn four times and مَنُون manūn once.

مَنَّ manna u [v. intrans.] 1 to bestow favours from a position of
strength or higher rank (3:164) لَقَدْ مَنَّ اللَّهُ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذْ بَعَثَ فِيهِمْ
رَسُولًا truly God was gracious to the believers when He sent to
them a Messenger from themselves; *(38:39) هَذَا عَطَاؤُنَا فَامْنُنْ أَوْ أَمْسِكْ
this is Our gift; do as you wish with it [lit. so bestow or withhold]
2 to remind someone of a favour, freely given, wishing to put
him/her under obligation, to recount one’s favours to someone
(49:17) يَمُنُّونَ عَلَيْكَ أَنْ أَسْلَمُوا قُلْ لَا تَمُنُّوا عَلَيَّ إِسْلَامَكُمْ بَلِ اللَّهُ يَمُنُّ عَلَيْكُمْ
أَنْ هَدَاكُمْ لِلْإِيمَانِ they count it as a favour to you [Prophet] that
they have become Muslims. Say, ‘Do not count your being
Muslims as a favour to me–it is God who reminds you of the

-ي

favour of his having guided you to the faith’ 3 to weaken, to
knuckle under (in an interpretation of 74:6) وَلَا تَمْنُنْ تَسْتَكْثِرُ and
never weaken, considering as too much [what you so far has been
commanded to do] (or, do not bestow favours seeking increased
returns).

مَنّ mann I [v. n.] 1 the act of freely bestowing favours (47:4)
حَتَّى إِذَا أَثْخَنْتُمُوهُمْ فَشُدُّوا الْوَثَاقَ فَإِمَّا مَنًّا بَعْدُ وَإِمَّا فِدَاءً until you have routed
them, then tie fast the bonds, thereafter releasing [them] by grace
or ransoming [them] 2 the act of reminding someone of favours
freely given wishing to put him under obligation (2:264) لَا تُبْطِلُوا
صَدَقَاتِكُمْ بِالْمَنِّ وَالْأَذَى do not negate your charitable deeds with
reminders and hurtful words/actions II [n.] manna, honey-like
substance, food given as a gift from God (2:57) وَأَنْزَلْنَا عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَنَّ
وَالسَّلْوَى and We sent manna and quails down to you.

مَمْنُون mamnūn [pass. part.] something grudged, interrupted,
decreased (41:8) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ لَهُمْ أَجْرٌ غَيْرُ مَمْنُونٍ those
who believe and do good deeds will have a reward that is never
interrupted/grudged.

مَنُون manūn [n.] bad turns of events, death *(52:30) رَيْبَ الْمَنُونِ
misfortune [lit. accident of death].
